question: The pop star behind "…Baby One More Time" has had her mental health journey play out in the public eye, particularly through a highly publicized conservatorship that led to global conversations about autonomy, disability, and power.

Blind since shortly after birth, this legendary singer and pianist became one of Motown's biggest stars, recording classics like "Superstition" and "Sir Duke" while redefining what accessibility in music could look like.
This German composer began losing his hearing in early adulthood and composed much of his later work while deaf, including the Ninth Symphony, which helped redefine Western classical music.
Blinded as a child, this musician blended jazz, blues, and gospel into a groundbreaking sound, earning the nickname "The Genius" and recording hits like "Georgia on My Mind."

This pop superstar has spoken openly about living with fibromyalgia and has incorporated disability imagery into her work, including the wheelchair-centered "Paparazzi" music video, and is known for boundary-pushing fashion moments like wearing a dress made of raw meat

In the world of Pokémon, Shiny characters are ultra-rare Pokémon with a unique color swap; most notably the red-scaled Gyarados as seen in the image here. However, for players with this specific type of color blindness, the vibrant red scales appear as a muddy brownish-gray, making the rare find look more identical to the common blue one.
answers: Achromatopsia (monochromacy) — no functioning cone cells at all, seeing only in shades of gray; Extremely rare and often accompanied by light sensitivity and reduced visual acuity.
Protanopia (a clinical term for color blindness that makes it difficult to perceive red)
Deuteranomaly — reduced green cone sensitivity; this is the most common type overall

question: Nintendo Switch Pokémon games allow for "Single-Handed Mode." Name a situational limitation where someone without a permanent impairment benefits from this.
answers: A player trying to increase the difficulty of the game's combat
A commuter holding onto a subway rail with one hand while playing
A user trying to conserve their controller's battery life
Playing the game while wearing a virtual reality (VR) headset

question: While often used by experienced players to skip through dialogue, this adjustable game setting is a vital accessibility tool that reduces 'Cognitive Load' for players with Dyslexia or other processing disorders.

This painter developed severe cataracts in both eyes, rendering him legally blind. This caused him to see colors in blurred yellows, brown, and reds, heavily influencing his abstract work "Water Lilies"
This famous Mexican painter used art to explore her experience with chronic pain and disability following a streetcar accident.

This famous painting is described by the following alternative text (courtesy of Blackboard Ally): Portrait of a woman with a subtle smile, wearing a Renaissance-style dress and sitting with her hands folded against a misty landscape background
This Blackboard product gives content an accessibility score and step-by-step guidance to improve items like color contrast, headings, and alternative text.
In Blackboard, it is more efficient for screen reader users to navigate by these, rather than using the tab key.

"I have struggled with every other screen capturing program I have tried. Using [this Blackboard product] was the first time I could start and stop a recording without help. It was liberating for me … job well done on this one!" –Visually impaired faculty member who uses JAWs to teach fully online.
When accessibility is everyone's responsibility, learning together matters. This group exists so anyone can get involved, grow their knowledge, and help move accessibility forward.
This invention brightens your smile and makes your hygienist happy!
This brand made everyone's cooking experience easier by creating accessibility-friendly kitchen accessories.
This tool is hygienically sensible, and makes it easier for everyone to finish a common cleaning task.
Making it easier for people to stay hydrated, this product was originally designed with accessibility in mind.

While finishing his dissertation at the University of Delaware, Wayne Westerman developed a severe case of carpal tunnel syndrome, thereby inhibiting his ability to use the computer effectively. So he and some colleagues created this commonly used piece of technology.
